proposals.reason: Swissmedic Swissdamed and TGA Device ID became mandatory on 1 July 2026, requiring immediate compliance for manufacturers targeting Swiss and Australian markets.

card.x: Swissdamed (Switzerland) and Device ID (Australia) systems now mandatory for all device registrations and market surveillance.

proposals.reason: New CNIL methodologies MR-001 and MR-003 published, establishing updated legal frameworks for clinical research data processing in France.

card.x: Updated reference methodologies for biomedical research, observational studies, and clinical trials involving personal data in France.

proposals.reason: MHRA published Phase 2 AI Airlock report documenting evaluation criteria and methodologies for medical AI products in the UK sandbox.

card.t: MHRA — AI Airlock Phase 2 Report Published
card.x: Evaluation findings from AI sandbox provide insight into UK regulatory expectations for algorithm validation and AI safety.

proposals.reason: MDCG 2026-4 clarifies that manufacturers alone are responsible for uploading SSP and SSCP summaries into EUDAMED, removing notified body involvement.

card.x: Manufacturers now exclusively responsible for uploading all SSP and SSCP summaries in EUDAMED; notified body role removed.

proposals.reason: Publication timeline for prEN 18286 (AI Act QMS standard) refined to Q4 2026, aiming to provide harmonised framework for Article 17 compliance.

card.t: prEN 18286 — AI Act QMS Standard Publication Expected
card.x: First harmonised standard for AI Act QMS (Article 17). Failed Jan 2026 vote; under revision. Target publication Q4 2026 to support presumption of conformity.

proposals.reason: ENISA published comprehensive report on SBOM application to medical devices, providing guidance ahead of CRA enforcement between 2027-2028.

card.x: Comprehensive analysis of Software Bill of Materials application to medical devices, with guidance for CRA compliance preparation.

proposals.reason: Clarified Master UDI-DI specifications and registration requirements for legacy devices ahead of 27 November 2026 deadline.

card.x: All legacy devices must complete Actor, UDI, Device, and Certificates modules registration including Master UDI-DI by this date.
